content:
# Numbers Chapter 6 — The SOPHIA Bible (Consensus)
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Source Manuscript:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 153–154; Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pa_0115–0116 (LXX) | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/NUM_006_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_006_page_002.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/NUM_006_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_006_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

## The Law of the Nazirite (vv. 1–8)

**6:1** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying:

**6:2** "Speak to the Sons of Israel and say to them: When a man or a woman makes an extraordinary vow, the vow of a Nazirite, to separate himself to YHWH,

**6:3** he shall separate himself from wine and strong drink. He shall not drink vinegar of wine or vinegar of strong drink, and he shall not drink any juice of grapes, and he shall not eat grapes, fresh or dried.

**6:4** All the days of his separation he shall not eat anything that is made from the grapevine, from the seeds even to the skin.

**6:5** All the days of the vow of his separation, no razor shall pass over his head. Until the days are fulfilled for which he separates himself to YHWH, he shall be holy; he shall let the locks of the hair of his head grow freely.

**6:6** All the days of his separation to YHWH he shall not come near to a dead person.

**6:7** For his father or for his mother, for his brother or for his sister -- he shall not make himself unclean for them when they die, for the consecration of his God is upon his head.

**6:8** All the days of his separation he is holy to YHWH.

---

## Defilement and Reconsecration of the Nazirite (vv. 9–12)

**6:9** And if anyone dies very suddenly beside him, and he defiles the head of his consecration, then he shall shave his head on the day of his cleansing; on the seventh day he shall shave it.

**6:10** And on the eighth day he shall bring two turtledoves or two young pigeons to the priest, to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting.

**6:11** And the priest shall offer one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering, and shall make atonement for him, because he sinned on account of the dead person. And he shall consecrate his head on that day.

**6:12** And he shall separate to YHWH the days of his separation, and shall bring a male lamb in its first year as a guilt offering. And the former days shall fall away, for his separation was defiled.

---

## Completion of the Nazirite Vow (vv. 13–21)

**6:13** And this is the law of the Nazirite: on the day when the days of his separation are fulfilled, he shall be brought to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting,

**6:14** and he shall present his offering to YHWH: one male lamb in its first year without blemish as a burnt offering, and one ewe lamb in its first year without blemish as a sin offering, and one ram without blemish as peace offerings,

**6:15** and a basket of unleavened bread, cakes of fine flour mixed with oil, and unleavened wafers anointed with oil, and their grain offering and their drink offerings.

**6:16** And the priest shall bring them before YHWH and shall offer his sin offering and his burnt offering.

**6:17** And the ram he shall offer as a sacrifice of peace offerings to YHWH, together with the basket of unleavened bread. And the priest shall offer its grain offering and its drink offering.

**6:18** And the Nazirite shall shave the head of his consecration at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting, and shall take the hair of the head of his consecration and put it on the fire that is under the sacrifice of the peace offerings.

**6:19** And the priest shall take the boiled shoulder of the ram, and one unleavened cake from the basket, and one unleavened wafer, and shall put them upon the palms of the Nazirite after he has shaved the head of his consecration.

**6:20** And the priest shall wave them as a wave offering before YHWH. It is holy for the priest, together with the breast of the wave offering and the thigh of the contribution. And after that the Nazirite may drink wine.

**6:21** This is the law of the Nazirite who vows his offering to YHWH for his separation, besides what his hand can attain. According to the vow which he vows, so he shall do, according to the law of his separation."

---

## The Aaronic Blessing (vv. 22–27)

**6:22** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying:

**6:23** "Speak to Aaron and to his sons, saying: Thus you shall bless the Sons of Israel; say to them:

**6:24** May YHWH bless you and keep you.

**6:25** May YHWH make His face shine upon you and be gracious to you.

**6:26** May YHWH lift up His face upon you and give you peace.

**6:27** And they shall put My name upon the Sons of Israel, and I Myself will bless them."
